_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in developing crisis action timelines
reference_document_element_description: Skill in designing and maintaining a model risk governance framework, including policy development, role and responsibility definition, escalation protocols, exception management, and board-level model risk reporting.
comments: NICE skill intersects with WIDAI RM domain. Skill RM-S-006 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RM-S-008 (RM, cosine: 0.457), LS-S-007 (LS, cosine: 0.444).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.1/10 (raw 0.409). Shared vocabulary: [skill]. NICE-only terms [action, crisis, developing, timelines] vs WIDAI-only terms [board, definition, designing, development, escalation, exception] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.884 (P=0.907, R=0.861). P exceeds R by 0.046 — the NICE element's 6 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 26 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (77.6%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Skill in developing crisis action timelines" and "Skill in designing and maintaining a model risk governance framework, including". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.467 > STS 0.409 (gap +0.057). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [skill] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 7.4%. Shared tokens: [skill]. NICE-only: [action, crisis, developing, timelines]. WIDAI-only: [board, definition, designing, development, escalation, exception].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 77.6% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
